architecture_adjudication.reason: This is materially different from the prior owned page-permission run because each history window performs equal owned-page recovery before a fixed no-recovery sentinel; the prior run only flipped permissions outside the measured sentinel.
architecture_adjudication.subagent_boundary: read-only architecture review; no checkout mutation, no lab-device contact, no Git writes
architecture_adjudication.next_boundary_if_negative: cheap closed CAT_CAS-owned carrier/access-model options appear exhausted; next useful moves require a changed authorization class

predeclared_experiment.carrier: CAT_CAS-owned anonymous user-space pages plus synchronous owned page-recovery state
predeclared_experiment.history_operator: neutral, forward, reverse, or shuffle recovery histories over four fixed page groups; each owned page recovers exactly once per history window
predeclared_experiment.sentinel: fixed owned-page read sequence G0-G1-G2-G3 with no recovery during the measured sentinel
predeclared_experiment.observable: owned_recovery_group PMU cache counters, cycles, and sentinel duration
predeclared_experiment.restoration_class: all owned pages end read-write, bytes unchanged, pages resident, handler counts equal, sentinel recovery count zero, and no escaped recovery

claim_ceiling: Owned synchronous page-recovery timing/PMU discriminator only; no private-data or cross-domain observation, path memory, coherence holonomy, OrbitState coupling, fold-odd recovery, target coupling, or Small Wall crossing claim
frontier_update: The owned synchronous page-recovery history carrier is negative under equal recovery-count and no-recovery sentinel controls. Do not rerun this neutral/forward/reverse/shuffle owned-recovery sequence unchanged.
